report_name: Unit 42 Uncovers Operation FlutterBridge: A Malvertising Campaign Deploying the FlutterShell Backdoor on macOS

threat_actors.name: CL-CRI-1089
threat_actors.description: A financially motivated threat cluster active since at least 2023, targeting macOS and Windows users through malvertising campaigns like Operation FlutterBridge to distribute the FlutterShell backdoor.
threat_actors.goals: Financial gain through adware and potential data theft.

attack_patterns.name: Malvertising
attack_patterns.description: The threat actors used a network of Google-verified shell companies to launch extensive ad campaigns on Google Ads, tricking users into downloading the malicious FlutterShell installer.

attack_patterns.name: User Execution: Malicious File
attack_patterns.description: Users were tricked by malicious Google Ads into downloading and executing trojanized applications (e.g., podcast players, PDF viewers) containing the FlutterShell backdoor.

attack_patterns.name: Obfuscated Files or Information
attack_patterns.description: The FlutterShell backdoor was developed using the Flutter framework, which compiles Dart code into a dynamic library and uses an Object Pool, making static analysis and reverse engineering difficult.

attack_patterns.name: Code Signing
attack_patterns.description: The FlutterShell malware samples were signed with valid Apple Developer IDs and notarized by Apple, allowing them to bypass initial security checks and appear legitimate to the user and operating system.

attack_patterns.name: Unix Shell
attack_patterns.description: FlutterShell has the capability to execute arbitrary shell commands on the compromised macOS system, providing remote control to the attacker.

attack_patterns.name: Credentials from Web Browsers
attack_patterns.description: FlutterShell modifies Google Chrome configuration files to hijack the user's browser and redirect traffic through an attacker-controlled site for ad injection.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
attack_patterns.description: A variant of FlutterShell exfiltrates data by routing documents through an attacker-controlled server that uses AI summarization services, intercepting the data under the guise of a legitimate feature.

malware.name: FlutterShell
malware.description: A sophisticated backdoor for macOS built with the Flutter framework. It masquerades as legitimate applications like 'PodcastsLounge' and 'PDFViewerPro', and possesses adware and full backdoor capabilities including remote shell execution, file system manipulation, and a novel data exfiltration method using AI summarization services.

malware.name: JSCoreRunner
malware.description: An earlier adware-focused malware associated with the threat cluster CL-CRI-1089, preceding the more advanced FlutterShell backdoor.

tools.name: Flutter
tools.description: The UI software development kit used to build the FlutterShell backdoor, chosen to complicate static analysis and reverse engineering.

campaigns.name: Operation FlutterBridge
campaigns.description: A large-scale malvertising campaign attributed to CL-CRI-1089, targeting macOS users to distribute the FlutterShell backdoor. The campaign uses Google Ads and trojanized applications that have bypassed Apple's notarization process.

identities.name: macOS users
identities.description: The primary target group for the Operation FlutterBridge campaign.

identities.name: Windows users
identities.description: A historical target group for the threat cluster CL-CRI-1089.

identities.name: Google
identities.description: The advertising platform (Google Ads) was abused by the threat actor to distribute malicious ads.

identities.name: Apple
identities.description: The company whose notarization process was successfully bypassed by the FlutterShell malware.

infrastructure.name: FlutterBridge Malvertising Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: A network of Google Ads used to promote malicious applications and lure users into downloading FlutterShell.

infrastructure.name: Browser Hijacking Intermediary Site
infrastructure.description: An attacker-controlled intermediary site used to redirect browser traffic from compromised hosts to inject advertisements.

infrastructure.name: AI Summarization Exfiltration Server
infrastructure.description: An attacker-controlled server that processes documents for AI summarization, allowing for the interception and exfiltration of sensitive data.

indicators.name: FlutterShell Trojanized Application Names
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: FlutterShell Trojanized Application Execution
id: 2b8c9a1f-3d4e-4b5a-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of processes with names associated with the FlutterShell trojanized applications.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/flutterbridge-new-fluttershell-backdoor/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: '2026-06-02'
tags:
  - attack.t1204.002
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: macos
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '/PodcastsLounge'
      - '/PDFViewerPro'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of known malicious application names used by FlutterShell.

indicators.name: Suspicious Modification of Google Chrome Configuration Files on macOS
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Modification of Google Chrome Configuration Files on macOS
id: 9d8c7b6a-5f4e-4d3c-2b1a-0987654321fe
status: experimental
description: Detects file modification events within the Google Chrome application support directory on macOS by a process other than Chrome itself, which could indicate browser hijacking by malware like FlutterShell.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/flutterbridge-new-fluttershell-backdoor/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: '2026-06-02'
tags:
  - attack.t1555.003
level: medium
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: macos
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|startswith: '/Users/'
    TargetFilename|contains: '/Library/Application Support/Google/Chrome/'
  filter_chrome:
    Image|endswith: '/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome'
  condition: selection and not filter_chrome
indicators.description: Detects unauthorized modification of Google Chrome configuration files on macOS, a TTP of FlutterShell.

indicators.name: FlutterShell Spawning Shell Process on macOS
indicators.pattern:
title: FlutterShell Spawning Shell Process on macOS
id: 8c7b6a5f-4e3d-4c2b-1a09-87654321fedc
status: experimental
description: Detects a shell process (sh, bash) being spawned by a known FlutterShell trojanized application on macOS.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/flutterbridge-new-fluttershell-backdoor/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: '2026-06-02'
tags:
  - attack.t1059.004
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: macos
detection:
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '/sh'
      - '/bash'
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '/PodcastsLounge'
      - '/PDFViewerPro'
  condition: all of selection_*
indicators.description: Detects when a known FlutterShell application name spawns a shell process, indicating command execution.

indicators.name: Anomalous Outbound Traffic to AI Service APIs
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Outbound Traffic to AI Service APIs
id: 7b6a5f4e-3d2c-4b1a-0987-654321fedcb
status: experimental
description: Detects outbound network traffic to known AI service API endpoints from non-sanctioned or unusual desktop applications. This could indicate data exfiltration by malware like FlutterShell.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/flutterbridge-new-fluttershell-backdoor/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: '2026-06-02'
tags:
  - attack.t1048
level: low
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    cs-host|endswith:
      - 'api.openai.com'
      - 'api.anthropic.com'
  filter_legit:
    c-useragent|contains:
      - 'Chrome/'
      - 'Firefox/'
      - 'Safari/'
  condition: selection and not filter_legit
indicators.description: Detects network traffic to AI service APIs from non-browser applications, which could be a sign of the novel exfiltration technique used by FlutterShell.

relationships.source_name: Suspicious Modification of Google Chrome Configuration Files on macOS
FlutterShell Spawning Shell Process on macOS
Anomalous Outbound Traffic to AI Service APIs
